title: RST-I
text: Which option best describes the OOP principle of Encapsulation in Java?
choices: Child inherits parent properties
Hide details, access through methods
Class can behave differently via methods
Creating multiple objects from a single class

title: RST-II
text: Which option best describes the principle of Inheritance in Java?
choices: Hide details, access through methods
Class can behave differently via methods
Creating multiple objects from a single class
A child class inheriting properties from a parent class

title: RST-III
text: Which option best describes the principle of Polymorphism in Java?
choices: Hide details, access through methods
Class can behave differently via methods
Creating multiple objects from a single class
A child class inheriting properties from a parent class

title: RST-IV
text: Which option best describes the principle of Abstraction in Java?
choices: Showing only necessary details and hiding unimportant ones
Creating a large number of objects
Always using public variables
Inheriting through multiple layers of classes

title: RST-V
text: Which of the following best describes a Class in Java?
choices: A real-world object in memory
A blueprint for creating objects
A type of loop structure
A method inside an object

title: RST-VI
choices: A subclass replacing a parent method
Multiple methods with the same name but different parameters
A method that runs only once
A method that cannot be changed

title: RST-VII
choices: Changing the name of a method
Child overrides parent's method
Combining multiple methods into one
Deleting a method from a class

title: RST-VIII
text: Which best describes the static keyword in a java object?
choices: Belongs to object
Belongs to class
Used only in constructors
Deleted after compilation

title: RST-IX
text: What is the main benefit of using Java Programming for OOP?
choices: Makes programs faster in every case
Makes code organized, easy to maintain, and reusable
No need to use variables
Replaces everything with loops

title: RST-X
text: Which of the following best describes a Constructor in Java?
choices: Constructor runs when an object is created
A method that must return an integer.
A method that can only be called manually.
A method that always prints output.

title: RST-XI
text: Which best describes the “this” keyword in Java class?
choices: A reference to the current object.
A reference to the parent class.
A reference to the main method.
A reference to all objects.

title: RST-XII
text: What is a "Generic" in Java (e.g., List<T>) used for?
choices: To speed up compilation.
To allow methods to return multiple values.
Improve compile-time type safety
To create dynamic arrays.

title: RST-XIII
text: Which of the following correctly describes the "protected" access modifier in Java?
choices: Accessible only within the same class.
Accessible only within the same package.
Package-wide and subclass access
Accessible from any class in any package.

title: RST-XIV
choices: Creating too many objects until the Heap is full.
Recursive calls without base case causing overflow
Accessing an array index that does not exist.
Dividing an integer by zero.

title: RST-XV
text: Which of the following is a key difference between an Interface and an Abstract Class?
choices: Interfaces cannot have constructors
A class can extend one abstract class only
Interface defines behavior; abstract class can implement some
Abstract classes can have non-public methods

title: RST-XVI
text: What is the difference between throw and throws?
choices: throw declares an exception; throws creates an exception.
throw creates an exception; throws declares an exception.
throw is for checked errors; throws is for unchecked errors.
throw handles the error; throws ignores the error.

title: RST-XVII
text: What happens when you catch an exception but leave the block empty?
choices: The program crashes immediately with an error.
The exception is ignored and the program continues.
The compiler will refuse to compile the code.
The exception is automatically printed to the console.
